task.title: Implement a Stateless Local Ollama Chat Endpoint for ruizTechStudio Using the Orin Nano Docker GPU Container

preflight.step: If the branch is not app-shell, stop and report the mismatch.
Read all relevant documentation in docs/.
Inspect package.json, Next.js config, TypeScript config, lint config, existing app/, lib/, and any current API route patterns.
Do not assume folder structure. Match the existing codebase style.

task.context: ruizTechStudio is a codebase intelligence studio. Its purpose is to help import/connect repos, scan them, visualize their structure, recover where the user left off, and extract reusable code assets. This task is only a small foundation piece: a backend route that lets studio-2 call Gio's private Orin Nano Ollama server.

stateless_definition.rule: The API route must not save chat messages.
The API route must not rely on server memory between requests.
The client must send the full messages array each time.
The endpoint simply validates the request, forwards it to Ollama, and returns the result.
Any future persistence must be handled elsewhere, not in this route.

environment_variables.note: Do not hardcode the Nano URL directly inside React components. Use environment variables. If .env.example exists, update it. If it does not exist, create or update the repo's existing env documentation pattern only after inspecting the repo.

preferred_architecture.note: If the repo already has a better existing structure, follow it and explain why.

requirement.title: Create a Typed Ollama Client Module

rules.rule: Read base URL from process.env.OLLAMA_GPU_BASE_URL.
Default to http://100.86.175.53:11435 only if the project's conventions allow safe local defaults. Otherwise require the env var and return a clear config error.
Expose a function like chatWithOllama(input).
Use fetch.
Call ${baseUrl}/api/chat.
Send JSON.
Use stream: false for now.
Apply a reasonable timeout using AbortController.
Return parsed JSON.
Handle non-2xx responses.
Handle network errors.
Never expose stack traces or internal env details to the client.
Define allowed model(s) — initially only qwen2.5:7b-instruct-q4_K_M.
Define the default model.
Define default options: num_gpu: 2, num_ctx: 1024, num_predict: 256.
Define max messages allowed.
Define max content length per message.
Define accepted roles: system, user, assistant.
Prevent arbitrary models/options from being passed directly by the browser unless explicitly allowed.

requirement.title: Create the Stateless Route Handler

rules.rule: Export POST.
Accept JSON shaped as: messages (required), model (optional), options (optional).
Treat messages as required.
Validate that messages is a non-empty array.
Validate each message role and content.
Default the model if omitted.
Reject disallowed models.
Merge safe user options with default policy options.
Do not allow the user to override unsafe values freely.
Force or preserve: stream: false, keep_alive: "5m", num_gpu: 2 unless the policy explicitly allows another value.

response_behavior.success: Return JSON with: ok: true, model, message, usage/timing fields if Ollama returns them, raw provider metadata only if safe/useful.
response_behavior.error: Missing env/config — return a clean message.
Ollama connection failure or non-2xx/error JSON — return sanitized error.

requirement.title: Add a Health Endpoint if Appropriate

rules.rule: Call ${OLLAMA_GPU_BASE_URL}/api/tags.
Return whether the Ollama server is reachable.
Return available model names.
Do not expose private infrastructure details beyond what is needed for local development.
If the repo already has a health/check route pattern, follow that instead.

requirement.title: Add Tests if the Project Already Has Test Tooling

rules.rule: Inspect the repo for Vitest/Jest/Playwright/etc. before adding anything.
Do not introduce a new testing framework unless the repo clearly lacks one and the task scope allows it.
Mock fetch — do not hit the real Nano in automated tests.

requirement.title: Add a Manual curl Test Section to Docs

rules.rule: Find the correct docs file or create a focused doc under docs/ if needed.
Document required env vars.
Document local endpoints: POST /api/ai/chat and GET /api/ai/health if implemented.
Provide an example curl against the Next.js backend, not directly against the Nano.
Include a reminder that the frontend must call the Next.js backend, not the Nano directly.
Include a reminder that this endpoint is stateless.

requirement.example_curl: curl -sS http://localhost:3000/api/ai/chat \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ --data-raw '{"messages":[{"role":"system","content":"You are a concise codebase analysis assistant."},{"role":"user","content":"Explain what a Next.js App Router project structure usually contains in about 100 words."}]}'

hard_boundaries.boundary: Do not add auth unless the repo already has middleware that requires it for API routes.
Do not make the browser call 100.86.175.53 directly.
Do not implement repo scanning in this task.
Do not implement embeddings in this task.

code_quality.rule: TypeScript must be strict and clean.
Use clear types for request/response shapes.
Maintain separation of concerns: route handler = HTTP boundary, ollama client = provider call, model policy = allowed/default config.
Use readable error helpers if the repo already has them.
Match existing formatting and linting conventions.
No console spam in production routes unless the repo already has a logger pattern.
If a centralized logger or response helper already exists, use it instead of creating a new pattern.
Do not bypass existing project conventions for API errors, validation, or logging.

post_implementation.step: Run npm install only if needed.
Run npm run lint if available.
Run npm run typecheck if available.
Run npm run dev manual smoke test if practical.
Run git diff --stat and summarize the changed files.
Run git status and report whether the working tree has only expected changes.

expected_response.item: Exact curl command to test it

task.outcome: A clean, stateless Next.js backend API route in studio-2 that forwards validated chat requests to Gio's private Orin Nano Docker GPU Ollama container at OLLAMA_GPU_BASE_URL, using the stable model qwen2.5:7b-instruct-q4_K_M with safe default options. The frontend should call this backend route, never the Nano directly. Streaming is intentionally excluded from this task. The endpoint must use stream: false only.

result:
giost@gio_HP_envy MINGW64 ~/CascadeProjects/projects/application-studio/studio-2 (app-shell) $ curl -sS http://localhost:3000/api/ai/chat \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ --data-raw '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Explain this repo."}]}' {"ok":true,"model":"qwen2.5:7b-instruct-q4_K_M","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"To explain a specific GitHub repository (repo), I would need the name or URL of the repository in question. Without that information, I can provide a general overview of what a typical GitHub repository contains and how it is structured.

A typical GitHub repository includes:

1. **README.md**: A file at the root of the repo that often provides an introduction to the project and explains how to set up and use it.
2. **Code Files**: The main files or directories that contain the source code for the project.
3. **Images/Assets**: If the project involves any graphical content, this folder will hold images, icons, or other assets used in the project.
4. **Tests**: Test scripts or files to ensure the software works as expected.
5. **Documentation**: Files containing detailed explanations about how the application works and how to use it.
6. **Contributing Guidelines**: Instructions for others who might want to contribute to the project.
7. **.gitignore**: A file that lists filenames or extensions that Git should ignore when committing files.

If you provide the name or URL of the specific repository, I can give a more detailed explanation about its contents and purpose."},"usage":{"prompt_tokens":34,"completion_tokens":237},"timing":{"total_duration":41140350197,"load_duration":3286740381,"prompt_eval_duration":723627000,"eval_duration":37126673000},"done":true,"done_reason":"stop"} giost@gio_HP_envy MINGW64 ~/CascadeProjects/projects/application-studio/studio-2 (app-shell) $
